mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC below 0.02 across all historical easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in over 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession expansion phase reflects strengthening factory utilization and capacity utilization, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns during post-recession expansions, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly boost aggregate wage income and consumer expenditure, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no predictive power for 5-day returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPI growth above 2.5 percent erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 2.5 percent coincides with positive 60-day returns in over 70 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic momentum, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in predicting 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Negative industrial production growth during a late-cycle peak indicates factory slowdowns and weakening business investment, driving negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during late-cycle peaks fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with a hit rate below 40 percent.
